text.title: Retry with Backoff and Antipatterns
text.summary: Exponential backoff retry helper with Retryable interface and the most common agent antipatterns.

rule.statement: Tag retryable errors via a Retryable() bool interface, not by string-sniffing. Retry loops must check IsRetryable(err) before sleeping — 4xx errors must never be retried.
rule.rationale: String matching on error messages is fragile and breaks on i18n or message changes. An explicit interface is type-safe and readable.

antipattern.description: data, _ := json.Marshal(user) — ignoring errors with blank identifier.
antipattern.reason: json.Marshal fails on channels, functions, and cyclic structures. Silently ignoring the error sends malformed JSON to the client.

antipattern.description: if err != nil { log.Printf(...); return err } — logging then returning.
antipattern.reason: Every intermediate layer that logs-and-returns produces a duplicate log line. Only the outermost handler should log.

antipattern.description: panic(err) to handle expected failures like "user not found".
antipattern.reason: Panics propagate up the call stack and may crash the entire server if not recovered. Use error returns for expected conditions.
These mean the client cancelled or the deadline passed. Retrying wastes resources. Check errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) before retry.

text.reference: Pre-commit gate: errcheck, golangci-lint, storage-sentinel leak check
